title: raycast/extensions
content:
Raycast Extensions: Supercharge Your Productivity with Custom Workflows

Raycast is a powerful command palette for macOS that lets you control your tools, automate tasks, and boost productivity—all with just a few keystrokes. The 'raycast/extensions' repository is the central hub for all extensions available in the Raycast Store. Here, developers and power users can find, contribute, and explore a wide variety of extensions that integrate with popular apps and services.

This repository not only hosts the source code for these extensions but also provides comprehensive documentation and examples for building your own. Whether you want to manage your calendar, control smart devices, or streamline your development workflow, Raycast extensions make it possible. The open-source nature of this repo encourages collaboration and innovation, making Raycast a customizable tool that adapts to your needs.

By contributing to or using these extensions, you join a vibrant community dedicated to making everyday computing faster, smarter, and more enjoyable.

title: HomeScript (& my talk on it)
content:
HomeScript: Command-Line Power for HomeKit Automation

HomeScript is a Python-based tool and API designed to bring the full power of HomeKit automation to your command line. With HomeScript, you can toggle Homebridge accessories, automate lighting, control RGB lights, adjust brightness, hue, and more—all without ever opening the Home app.

Key features include easy name matching (so you don't have to remember exact accessory names), group actions for controlling multiple devices at once, and a fully importable API for advanced scripting. HomeScript is perfect for tinkerers, automation enthusiasts, and anyone who wants to integrate HomeKit devices into custom workflows or cron jobs.

The project is open source, supports Python 3+, and is actively seeking contributors. Its robust documentation and example scripts make it easy to get started, while advanced users can dive deep into automation and integration with other smart home tools.

Learn more and join the community: https://github.com/menahishayan/HomeScript

title: Home-Assistant-TP-Link-Router
content:
Home Assistant TP-Link Router Integration: Complete Router Control

This open source Home Assistant component brings full-featured router administration to TP-Link Archer C50 and N600 routers. With this integration, users can restart their router, enable or disable WAN and WiFi bands, monitor connected devices, and track clients by MAC address—all from the Home Assistant dashboard.

Installation is simple via HACS or manual setup, and the integration is based on the TP-Link Router API for maximum compatibility. The project is ideal for smart home enthusiasts who want to automate network management, monitor device connectivity, or trigger automations based on network events.

The repository includes detailed setup instructions, troubleshooting tips, and a welcoming call for contributors. If you want to extend your Home Assistant setup to include network-level automations, this integration is a must-have.

Explore the project: https://github.com/menahishayan/Home-Assistant-TP-Link-Router

title: HomeAssistant-Cupertino-Icons
content:
Home Assistant Cupertino Icons: Apple-Style Icons for Your Smart Home

Bring the elegance of Apple's SF Symbols to your Home Assistant dashboards with the HomeAssistant-Cupertino-Icons integration. Featuring over 3,000 icons, including support for multi-color and full-color variants, this project lets you create beautiful, modern interfaces for your smart home.

The integration is easy to install via HACS or manually, and supports Home Assistant's icon picker for seamless customization. Updated to SF Symbols v3.0, it ensures you have access to the latest iconography for all your devices and automations.

Whether you're building a custom dashboard or just want your smart home to look as good as it works, Cupertino Icons is the perfect addition.

Get started and browse the icon library: https://github.com/menahishayan/HomeAssistant-Cupertino-Icons

title: getsentry/sentry-docs
content:
Sentry Docs: Open Source Documentation for Error Monitoring and Performance

The 'sentry-docs' repository is the backbone of Sentry's comprehensive documentation, powering docs.sentry.io. Sentry is a leading platform for real-time error tracking and performance monitoring, trusted by developers worldwide.

This open source repo is built with Next.js and maintained by a large, active community. It contains guides, API references, and best practices for integrating Sentry into a wide range of applications and frameworks. Contributors help keep the docs up-to-date, accurate, and accessible, making it easier for developers to adopt and succeed with Sentry.

If you're passionate about documentation, developer experience, or just want to help others build better software, contributing to sentry-docs is a great way to make an impact.

Read the docs or get involved: https://github.com/getsentry/sentry-docs
